{"type":"idle_notification","from":"code-engineer","timestamp":"2026-05-27T16:39:59.900Z","idleReason":"available"}

PM work done — auto mode, so I made the design calls instead of stopping for a clarification round. Spec is filed as Task #1 and assigned to `code-engineer`.

**What's being built:** For every pipeline run, snapshot the raw `company_configurations` Mongo doc onto the `automation_runs` document at run-creation time, expose it on the single-run DevOps API only, and add a "View Config" link on the DevOps RunCard that opens a read-only JSON dialog.

**Key decisions I made (flag for user if any are wrong — easy to redirect now, harder later):**

1. **Snapshot timing = run-start only.** Captured in `RunTracker.__init__` from `container.raw_config`. Never updated mid-run. Mid-run config edits don't retro-apply.
2. **Snapshot content = raw Mongo doc** (pre-`from_dict`), minus `_id`/`__v`/`created_at`/`updated_at`. We store what the run actually read, not the parsed dataclass form — round-trip-safe and human-comparable to the live config.
3. **Pipeline orchestrator only.** Standalone scripts (`refresh_acme_data`, `migrate_prod_to_*`, etc.) don't load a `CompanyConfig` and won't get a snapshot. The "View Config" link is hidden for them (and for older runs without the field).
4. **List endpoints don't return the snapshot** — configs can be tens of KB and would bloat `runs/index.ts` and `latest-per-company.ts` payloads. UI fetches on-demand from `runs/[runId].ts` when the user clicks View Config.
5. **No backfill** of historical runs. They render unchanged, no "View Config" link.
6. **Display = read-only JSON dialog.** No diff view between runs, no edit, no "rerun with this config" — those are out of scope for v1.

**One thing worth checking with the user:** the request said "set and used" — I interpreted that as a single snapshot of the **active** config when the run starts (what `Container` loads from `company_configurations`). If they meant something else by "set" (e.g. CLI overrides, env-var overrides, the per-run `start_from_step` etc.), the spec would need to broaden. Worth confirming.

Engineer has the full task with acceptance criteria, file list, edge cases, and test plan. Reviewer should be queued after.
summary: Spec drafted and handed to engineer

Good news — the user's clarification matches the spec I already drafted (filed as Task #1 in the previous turn, owned by `code-engineer`). All three of their points are covered:

1. **Immutable snapshot at kickoff** — already speced; snapshot is written in `RunTracker.__init__` from `container.raw_config` and never updated. The explicit test in the plan mutates the live config after the run finishes and verifies the snapshot is unchanged.
2. **Storage on the runs collection** — already speced; new `company_configuration_snapshot` field on `TAutomationRun` (Mixed, optional).
3. **Button next to each run** — speced as a footer link originally; **I just moved it to the card header row** to match the user's exact wording ("on the top but next to each run"). Icon button next to the existing StopCircle cancel button, hidden when there's no snapshot (older runs, standalone runs).

No clarifying questions needed — engineer can proceed. I've pinged them with the diff. Reviewer should still queue after.

Review of company_configuration snapshot feature is complete. Sent findings to code-engineer.

**Blocker (1):** `View Config` link in `RunCard` will never render. `hasConfigSnapshot` checks `run.company_configuration_snapshot !== undefined`, but the two list endpoints that feed `RunCard` (`runs/index.ts`, `runs/latest-per-company.ts`) explicitly strip the field from their response shape (per the spec, for payload-size reasons). RunCard is only used from list contexts, so the link, dialog, and fetch handler are unreachable code. Fix: have the list endpoints emit a cheap `has_company_configuration_snapshot: boolean` flag and gate the link on that instead.

**Should-fix (2):**
- List endpoints don't project away the snapshot, so Mongo still ships full snapshot docs into the API process on every list call even though they're stripped from the response — wasted bandwidth/memory at scale.
- Engineer's handoff claimed "Ruff: All checks passed!" but a new E501 was introduced in `container.py:41` (the new comment is 95 chars vs `arcum_lib`'s 88-char limit). 3 other E501s on the same file are pre-existing.

**Nits (2):** dialog could show "no snapshot recorded" when null; ObjectId in dialog title is ugly. Both optional.

**Good:** Python plumbing is clean, schema choice (`Mixed`, optional) is right, strip-list (`_id`/`__v`/timestamps) is correct, CHANGELOG follows arcum_lib format, pyright passes.

Engineer is fixing B1 (and hopefully S1 + S2) and will hand back.

{"type":"idle_notification","from":"code-quality-reviewer","timestamp":"2026-05-27T16:50:44.484Z","idleReason":"available","summary":"[to code-engineer] Review findings: 1 blocker, 2 should-fix, 2 nits"}

Second review of the snapshot feature is done. Sent findings to code-engineer.

The PM updated the spec (task #3) to rename `company_configuration_snapshot` → `run_config_snapshot`, nest under `{ company_configuration, overrides: { only_step, stop_after_step, only_script, preserve_predictions } }`, and add a two-section dialog. The engineer's handoff message still describes the OLD design and the code mostly matches that older design. The only piece of the new spec that landed is the TypeScript type declaration — schema, Python writes, API field name, RunCard reads, and dialog rendering all still reference the old single-field shape. The new type is dead.

**Blockers (2):**
- B1: type vs. schema vs. write vs. read are all desynced — `TAutomationRun.run_config_snapshot` is declared but never populated, never queried, never displayed. Six code paths still use the old name/shape.
- B2: my first-review blocker is still open — `hasConfigSnapshot` in RunCard gates on `run.company_configuration_snapshot !== undefined`, but both list endpoints that mount RunCard explicitly omit that field, so the "View Config" icon never renders. Moving the link to a header IconButton was the only visual change.

**Should-fix (3):**
- S1: list endpoints still don't project away the snapshot at the Mongo layer (open from first review).
- S2: engineer's "All checks passed!" claim — they ran ruff with `--config pyproject.toml` from `src/ingestion/`, which uses the parent config that ignores E501. The nested `arcum_lib/pyproject.toml` (line-length 88, no E501 ignore) flags the new comment on container.py:41 as before. CLAUDE rules require the nested config gate.
- S3: CHANGELOG entry still describes the old design and field name.

I asked them to: (a) commit to one field name and propagate, (b) have the orchestrator build the overrides dict and pass it bundled with the company config, (c) update schema/API/RunCard/dialog to match, (d) add list-endpoint presence flag for B2, (e) fix the lint, (f) update CHANGELOG.

Heads up: the engineer's handoff messages have twice now claimed "All checks passed!" when only the parent ruff config passes. Worth nudging them to use the nested config command from `src/ingestion/arcum_lib/CLAUDE.md` going forward.
